name: Kentucky
description: Produces roughly 95% of the world's bourbon. Limestone-filtered water, iron-free and calcium-rich, plus hot summers and cold winters that push whiskey deep into charred oak.

name: Tennessee
description: Tennessee whiskey must be made in-state and charcoal-mellowed before barreling — the Lincoln County Process that defines Jack Daniel's.

name: Texas
description: Brutal heat swings age spirits at double speed; Texas is also home to the craft distilling boom that made Tito's a giant.

name: Vermont
description: Farm distilling country — WhistlePig's rye farm sits on 500 acres of Vermont pasture.

name: Washington
description: Pacific Northwest maritime climate and world-class barley have made Seattle a cradle of American single malt.

name: Napa Valley
description: America's most famous wine appellation: volcanic and alluvial soils, warm days and cool nights, and cabernet sauvignon of global renown.

name: California
description: From Sonoma's craft breweries to the Central Coast's vineyards, California anchors American drinks culture.

name: Speyside
description: The densest concentration of distilleries on earth, clustered along the River Spey. Style: orchard fruit, honey, malt — often sherry-influenced.

name: Highlands
description: Scotland's largest region, from heathery mainland malts to muscular coastal drams.

name: Islay
description: A small Hebridean island with an outsized reputation: peat bogs and Atlantic gales give Islay malts their smoke, iodine and sea-spray signature.

name: Islands
description: Skye, Orkney, Mull, Jura and Arran — maritime malts with peat, brine and heather.

name: Campbeltown
description: Once 'the whisky capital of the world' with 30+ distilleries; today just three survive, making funky, briny, oily malts collectors chase.

name: Lowlands
description: Gentle, grassy, often triple-distilled malts — plus the giant grain distilleries that feed Scotland's blends.

name: County Cork
description: Home of Midleton Distillery, where Jameson, Redbreast, and the entire single pot still revival are made.

name: County Antrim
description: Northern coastal county holding Bushmills, licensed in 1608 — the oldest licensed distillery site in the world.

name: Osaka Prefecture
description: Yamazaki, Japan's first malt whisky distillery, sits at the misty confluence of three rivers between Osaka and Kyoto.

name: Yamanashi Prefecture
description: Alpine forest at the foot of Mt. Kaikoma — home of Hakushu, one of the highest distilleries in the world.

name: Yamaguchi Prefecture
description: Rural western Honshu, where Asahi Shuzo mills rice to 23% for Dassai's flagship daiginjo.

name: Niigata Prefecture
description: Japan's snow country: soft meltwater and cold winters yield the clean, dry 'tanrei karakuchi' sake style.

name: Manitoba
description: Prairie wheat country on the shore of Lake Winnipeg — every drop of Crown Royal is distilled at Gimli.

name: Ontario
description: Windsor's Hiram Walker plant, across the river from Detroit, is the largest beverage-alcohol distillery in North America.

name: Cognac
description: Chalky Charente vineyards, ugni blanc grapes, double distillation in Charentais copper pot stills, and aging in Limousin oak: the world's benchmark brandy.

name: Armagnac
description: Gascony's rustic elder sibling to cognac — single continuous distillation to lower proof keeps more fruit and character.

name: Champagne
description: Chalk soils at the northern edge of viable viticulture. Only sparkling wine from this region, made by the traditional method, may bear the name.

name: Bordeaux
description: The world's largest fine-wine region; the 1855 Classification still ranks its left-bank châteaux, with Margaux among the five First Growths.

name: Burgundy
description: Pinot noir and chardonnay parsed into hundreds of climats — the most terroir-obsessed wine region on earth.

name: Provence
description: France's oldest wine region and the world capital of pale, dry rosé.

name: Normandy
description: Apple and pear orchards replace vineyards; the resulting cider and calvados carry their own appellations.

name: Alsace
description: Aromatic white wine country along the Rhine — riesling and gewurztraminer in tall flûte bottles.

name: Jalisco
description: Blue Weber agave country: the volcanic lowlands around Tequila town and the red-clay Highlands (Los Altos) define the two poles of tequila style.

name: Oaxaca
description: Where mezcal remains village craft: agave hearts pit-roasted over hot stones, crushed by stone tahona, and distilled in tiny palenques.

name: Mosel
description: Vertiginous slate slopes above the winding Mosel river produce rieslings of unmatched delicacy, tension and longevity.

name: Black Forest
description: Schwarzwald spruce, spring water and foraged botanicals — the unlikely home of Monkey 47 gin.

name: Tuscany
description: Sangiovese heartland; when Antinori bottled Tignanello outside DOC rules, the 'super Tuscan' category was born.

name: Veneto
description: Glera grapes and tank-method sparkle made Prosecco the world's best-selling bubbly; Veneto is also home to Luxardo's cherry orchards.

name: Lombardy
description: Milan is the birthplace of the aperitivo hour — Campari red has colored its cafés since 1860.

name: Catalonia
description: Penedès vineyards produce cava by the traditional method from macabeo, xarel·lo and parellada.

name: Jerez
description: Albariza chalk, palomino grapes, and the magic of flor yeast aging under a veil in ancient soleras.

name: Douro Valley
description: The world's first legally demarcated wine region: terraced schist gorges whose grapes become port, fortified with grape spirit and aged in Vila Nova de Gaia lodges.

name: West Flanders
description: Home to Westvleteren's Saint-Sixtus abbey, where monks sell beer by appointment only at the gate.

name: Hainaut
description: Rolling farmland along the French border, home of Chimay's Scourmont Abbey and its Trappist brewery.

name: Gaume
description: Belgium's sunny southern corner, where Orval's monks brew a single, Brettanomyces-laced beer.

name: Marlborough
description: Sunshine, stony soils and cool nights at the top of the South Island: the region that made sauvignon blanc explosive.

name: Yilan County
description: Snowmelt from the Central Mountains and subtropical humidity make Kavalan's warehouses some of the fastest-aging on earth.

name: Karnataka
description: Amrut distills at 3,000 feet in Bangalore, where tropical heat triples the pace of barrel aging — and the angels' share.

name: Trelawny Parish
description: The funk capital of rum: Hampden Estate's wild fermentations produce the highest-ester rums in the world.

name: Saint Elizabeth Parish
description: The lush Nassau Valley, 'the greatest rum-making valley on earth', home of Appleton Estate since 1749.

name: Saint Lucy Parish
description: Barbados' northern tip, where Mount Gay has drawn coral-filtered water since 1703.

name: Saint Michael Parish
description: Bridgetown's parish hosts the West Indies Rum Distillery, source of many celebrated Bajan blends.

name: Demerara
description: The river that named a sugar and a rum style; Diamond Distillery preserves the world's last working wooden stills.
